label: Time Horizons
description: Canonical temporal windows used for evidence interpretation, EO execution and outcome anchoring.
usageNotes: Use `code` as the persisted normalized horizon.
Detailed temporal offsets can be stored separately if exact timestamps are known.

entries.label: Immediate
entries.description: Immediate horizon before elapsed time accumulates.
usageNotes: Use for triggers that are actioned at recognition.

entries.label: Intraoperative
entries.description: Horizon limited to the ongoing operative or procedural encounter.
usageNotes: Use only during the active procedure.

entries.label: Postoperative 24h
entries.description: First 24 hours after surgery or procedure.
usageNotes: Preferred over generic short-term when the first day matters.

entries.label: Postoperative 48h
entries.description: First 48 hours after surgery or procedure.

entries.label: Index Hospitalization
usageNotes: Use for LOS, bundle adherence and discharge readiness.

entries.label: ICU Stay
entries.description: Duration of ICU admission or ICU phase.
usageNotes: Use when timing is ICU-bounded rather than hospital-bounded.

entries.label: 7 Days
usageNotes: Use for early complications or rescue endpoints.

entries.label: 90 Days
usageNotes: Preferred when postoperative effects extend beyond 30 days.

entries.label: 6 Months
usageNotes: Useful for medium-term recovery and symptom outcomes.

entries.label: 1 Year
usageNotes: Common horizon for durable clinical endpoints.

entries.label: Long-term
entries.description: Long-term horizon beyond fixed short and medium checkpoints.
usageNotes: Use when evidence reports durable or lifetime effects without a single standard cutpoint.
